title: Add a pinned, hash-locked dependency manifest plus pip-audit supply-chain gate
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 — LLM03 Supply Chain Vulnerabilities; PyPA pip-audit / OSV; SLSA hashed-requirements guidance.
description: The project has no requirements file, lockfile, or pyproject.toml, yet imports third-party packages (mcp SDK, jsonschema). Dependencies are resolved implicitly from the ambient environment with no version pinning, hash verification, or vulnerability scanning — leaving the orchestrator open to dependency-confusion and known-CVE pulls, the core LLM03 risk for a system that shells out to `claude` and an MCP server.
proposed_changes: Generate a pinned `requirements.lock` via `pip-compile --generate-hashes` (or `uv pip compile`) capturing mcp, jsonschema, and transitive deps with SHA-256 hashes; document `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock` as the install path. Add a `supply-chain-check` subcommand to security_utils.py that runs `pip-audit -r requirements.lock` and surfaces CVEs via audit_log_security_event(). Optionally invoke it from the Prometheus scan cycle so a known-vulnerable dependency is flagged as a finding rather than silently shipped.
